interaction: Paused manual frequency tuning; memory after first contact; incoming CALL indicator. MG2 adds the numeric keypad and dual portraits.
visual: Hard pixel interface. MG uses a green transceiver panel; MG2 uses a blue-gray radio screen and keypad.
audio: Short square-wave pulses and repeating incoming-call cadence.

interaction: Manual frequency tuning, CALL, MEMORY and incoming calls while gameplay is paused.
visual: Black and phosphor-green twin portrait composition with central frequency display and CRT breakup.
audio: Two-tone connection cues, frequency ticks and repeating CALL signal.

interaction: Frequency contacts, save channels, camera movement and positive/negative pressure responses during calls.
visual: Green monochrome illustrated twin portraits with central frequency and restrained digital framing.
audio: Cleaner, shorter digital pulses than MGS1.

interaction: MEM, SEND and TUNE controls; manual frequencies; personal-data pages and special healing/fire-support frequencies.
visual: Muted grayscale/olive radio overlay with a small portrait, central frequency and page index rather than a physical radio prop.
audio: Dry analog tuning clicks, narrow-band hiss and slower radio connection tones.

device: Metal Gear Mk. II / Nomad secure video link
interaction: Contextual secure video calls rather than classic manual frequency tuning.
visual: Wide, fully modeled video-call spaces with scene context and lip-synced character feeds.
audio: Low-noise encrypted link pulses and restrained network ambience.

interaction: Non-pausing battlefield Codec plus pre-mission audio briefing library.
visual: Mission-select/briefing-file hierarchy with olive MSF panels and cassette-oriented file playback.
audio: Portable-radio chatter for live calls and tape transport character for briefings.

interaction: Real-time mission device, map/intel routing and cassette playback; no classic frequency Codec.
visual: Blue-green transparent iDroid panels, left-side module list, cassette rows, waveform and persistent field metrics.
audio: iDroid activation pulse, compressed radio chatter and cassette transport noise.

visual: Grid-based synthetic presentation intentionally separated from canon hardware.

interaction: Late-MGS2-inspired AI interruption mode, explicitly marked as simulation.
visual: MGS2 green signal corrupted by white/red errors and contradictory readouts.
audio: Unstable detuned pulses and broken buffer noise.
